title: grindelia squarrosa var. serrulata
intro: Grindelia squarrosa var. serrulata, also known as Serrate Grindelia or Gum Plant, is a plant species in the daisy family that is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which can help soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Grindelia squarrosa var. serrulata include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as digestive problems. Herbal preparations of Grindelia squarrosa var. serrulata include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and other respiratory conditions.
